title: Early Moments in Rock Music History
titleZh: 摇滚乐历史的早期时刻

q.en: Who gave rock 'n' roll its name, and where and when did he start playing it?
q.zh: 是谁给摇滚乐起了'rock 'n' roll'这个名字？他在何时何地开始播放这种音乐？

options.en: A disc jockey named Alan Freed, on a Cleveland radio station beginning in 1951.
options.zh: 一位名叫艾伦·弗里德（Alan Freed）的唱片骑师，于1951年开始在克利夫兰的一家电台播放。

options.en: Elvis Presley, in Memphis in 1955.
options.zh: 埃尔维斯·普雷斯利（猫王），1955年在孟菲斯。

options.en: Chuck Berry, in St. Louis in 1958.
options.zh: 查克·贝里（Chuck Berry），1958年在圣路易斯。

options.en: The Beatles, in Liverpool in 1960.
options.zh: 披头士乐队，1960年在利物浦。

explain.en: The book says the term 'rock 'n' roll' 'came from a disc jockey named Alan Freed, who started playing early rock music on a Cleveland radio station beginning in 1951.'
explain.zh: 书中说'rock 'n' roll'一词'来自一位名叫艾伦·弗里德的唱片骑师，他于1951年开始在克利夫兰的一家电台播放早期摇滚乐'。

q.en: Who invented the electric guitar, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据本书，谁发明了电吉他？

options.en: Adolph Rickenbacker.
options.zh: 阿道夫·里肯巴克（Adolph Rickenbacker）。

options.en: Les Paul.
options.zh: 莱斯·保罗（Les Paul）。

options.en: Sam Phillips.
options.zh: 萨姆·菲利普斯（Sam Phillips）。

options.en: Dick Clark.
options.zh: 迪克·克拉克（Dick Clark）。

explain.en: The book says, 'Adolph Rickenbacker invented the electric guitar,' which was plugged into an amplifier to make the sound louder.
explain.zh: 书中说：'阿道夫·里肯巴克发明了电吉他'，它插上放大器后能让声音更响亮。

q.en: Which 1957 TV show featured teenagers dancing to the Top 40 hits?
q.zh: 哪个1957年的电视节目以青少年随着'Top 40'热门歌曲跳舞为特色？

options.en: Dick Clark's American Bandstand.
options.zh: 迪克·克拉克的《美国舞台》（American Bandstand）。

options.en: The Ed Sullivan Show.
options.zh: 《埃德·沙利文秀》（The Ed Sullivan Show）。

options.en: Leave It to Beaver.
options.zh: 《留给海狸》（Leave It to Beaver）。

options.en: The Adventures of Ozzie and Harriet.
options.zh: 《奥齐与哈丽特历险记》（The Adventures of Ozzie and Harriet）。

explain.en: The book says, 'TV programs like Dick Clark's American Bandstand (1957) became a big draw to teenagers. Bandstand featured teenagers dancing to Top 40 hits.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'像迪克·克拉克的《美国舞台》（1957）这样的电视节目对青少年很有吸引力。该节目以青少年随着Top 40热门歌曲跳舞为特色。'

q.en: What title is Elvis Presley often given in the history of rock music?
q.zh: 在摇滚乐历史上，埃尔维斯·普雷斯利常被称为什么？

options.en: The 'King of Rock and Roll.'
options.zh: '摇滚乐之王'（King of Rock and Roll）。

options.en: The 'Duke of Folk.'
options.zh: '民谣公爵'。

options.en: The 'Prince of Jazz.'
options.zh: '爵士王子'。

options.en: The 'Father of the Electric Guitar.'
options.zh: '电吉他之父'。

explain.en: The book says Elvis 'is another important personality in the history of rock music. So important, in fact, that many have crowned him the 'King of Rock and Roll.''
explain.zh: 书中说埃尔维斯'是摇滚乐史上另一位重要人物。事实上他如此重要，以至于许多人加冕他为'摇滚乐之王'。'

q.en: What happened to Buddy Holly and Ritchie Valens in 1959?
q.zh: 1959年，巴迪·霍利（Buddy Holly）和里奇·瓦伦斯（Ritchie Valens）发生了什么？

options.en: They died in a plane crash on their way to a gig in Moorhead, Minnesota.
options.zh: 他们在前往明尼苏达州穆尔黑德演出的途中死于飞机失事。

options.en: They won a music award together on TV.
options.zh: 他们一起在电视上赢得了音乐奖。

options.en: They started a new band called The Beatles.
options.zh: 他们组建了一支名为披头士的新乐队。

options.en: They retired from music to become farmers.
options.zh: 他们退出乐坛去当了农民。

explain.en: The book says, 'They died in a plane crash in 1959 on their way to a gig in Moorhead, Minnesota.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'1959年，他们在前往明尼苏达州穆尔黑德演出的途中死于飞机失事。'

q.en: What famous stage move did Chuck Berry introduce, and what was one of his best-known songs?
q.zh: 查克·贝里引入了什么著名的舞台动作？他最知名的歌曲之一是哪首？

options.en: The 'duck walk,' and his song 'Johnny B. Goode' (1958).
options.zh: '鸭子步'（duck walk），以及他的歌曲《Johnny B. Goode》（1958）。

options.en: The curled lip, and the song 'Peggy Sue.'
options.zh: '卷曲的嘴唇'，以及歌曲《Peggy Sue》。

options.en: The hiccup in singing, and the song 'La Bamba.'
options.zh: 唱歌时的'打嗝'，以及歌曲《La Bamba》。

options.en: The moonwalk, and the song 'That's All Right, Mama.'
options.zh: '太空步'，以及歌曲《That's All Right, Mama》。

explain.en: The book says Berry 'introduced stage antics into his performances, like the famous 'duck walk'' and that 'One of Berry's most well-known songs was Johnny B. Goode (1958).'
explain.zh: 书中说贝里'将舞台噱头引入表演，比如著名的'鸭子步''，并且'贝里最知名的歌曲之一是《Johnny B. Goode》（1958）。'

q.en: How did the invention of the transistor change the way teenagers listened to rock 'n' roll?
q.zh: 晶体管的发明如何改变了青少年听摇滚乐的方式？

options.en: The transistor radio was small enough to fit in a pocket, letting teens listen to rock away from their parents and choose their own stations.
options.zh: 晶体管收音机小到可以放进口袋，让青少年能远离父母听摇滚乐，并选择自己喜欢的电台。

options.en: It made records play at 78 RPM instead of 45 RPM.
options.zh: 它让唱片以78转而非45转播放。

options.en: It replaced guitars with computers.
options.zh: 它用计算机取代了吉他。

options.en: It made radios as big as furniture.
options.zh: 它让收音机变得像家具一样大。

explain.en: The book says the transistor radio 'made it possible for teenagers to listen to rock 'n' roll music away from their parents' and that pocket transistor radios 'were small enough to fit in a shirt pocket.'
explain.zh: 书中说晶体管收音机'让青少年能够远离父母听摇滚乐'，且口袋晶体管收音机'小到可以放进口衣袋'。

q.en: According to the book, how did rock 'n' roll help break down racial barriers in the 1950s?
q.zh: 根据本书，摇滚乐在1950年代如何帮助打破种族壁垒？

options.en: White and black teenagers listened to the same music and watched the same shows, helping integrate blacks and whites and protest segregation.
options.zh: 白人和黑人青少年听同样的音乐、看同样的节目，帮助黑人与白人融合，并抗议种族隔离。

options.en: It made all radios the same color.
options.zh: 它让所有收音机变成同一种颜色。

options.en: It ended the Great Depression.
options.zh: 它结束了大萧条。

options.en: It made television illegal in the South.
options.zh: 它使电视在南方成为非法。

explain.en: The book says 'Rock 'n' roll music helped integrate blacks and whites because it helped break down the walls of segregation,' as teens of both races enjoyed the same music.
explain.zh: 书中说'摇滚乐帮助黑人与白人融合，因为它打破了隔离的围墙'，因为两个种族的青少年都喜爱同样的音乐。

q.en: In this book's glossary, what does the word 'rockabilly' mean?
q.zh: 在本书词汇表中，'rockabilly' 一词是什么意思？

options.en: A style of music that combines elements of rock 'n' roll with elements of country and western music.
options.zh: 一种将摇滚乐元素与乡村西部音乐元素相结合的风格。

options.en: A type of radio used by teenagers.
options.zh: 一种青少年使用的收音机。

options.en: A dance move performed on stage.
options.zh: 在舞台上表演的一种舞蹈动作。

options.en: A kind of electric guitar.
options.zh: 一种电吉他。

explain.en: The glossary defines 'rockabilly' as 'style of music that combines elements of rock 'n' roll with elements of country and western music' (p. 15).
explain.zh: 词汇表把 rockabilly 定义为'将摇滚乐元素与乡村西部音乐元素相结合的风格'（第15页）。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To inform readers about the early history of rock 'n' roll—its origins, technology, performers, and cultural impact.
options.zh: 向读者介绍摇滚乐的早期历史——它的起源、技术、表演者及文化影响。

options.en: To persuade readers to buy more records.
options.zh: 劝说读者购买更多唱片。

options.en: To teach readers how to play the guitar.
options.zh: 教读者弹吉他。

options.en: To tell a made-up story about a band.
options.zh: 讲述一个关于乐队虚构的故事。

explain.en: The book covers audience, technology (records, radio, TV, electric guitar), performers, and society/culture, showing it is meant to inform about rock's early history.
explain.zh: 本书涵盖了听众、技术（唱片、收音机、电视、电吉他）、表演者以及社会/文化，说明其旨在介绍摇滚乐的早期历史。

prompt.en: The book says rock 'n' roll grew from many styles—rhythm and blues, gospel, country, and more—and from both black and white musicians. Why do you think mixing different styles and people helped rock 'n' roll become so popular with teenagers?
prompt.zh: 书中说摇滚乐源自许多风格——节奏布鲁斯、福音、乡村等——并来自黑人和白人音乐人。你认为融合不同风格和不同人群，为何能帮助摇滚乐在青少年中如此流行？

en: Look at the 'Performers' section about white artists remaking rhythm and blues from black artists.
Think about the 'Culture' section and how rock helped bring black and white teens together.
zh: 看'表演者'一节，关于白人艺术家翻唱黑人艺术家的节奏布鲁斯。
想想'文化'一节，以及摇滚如何帮助黑人与白人青少年走到一起。
